PricingPlan.title: PricingPlan
PricingPlan.description: Represents a pricing plan for the hospital management system, including its name, price, features, and other promotional details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PricingPlan entity.

name.description: The name of the pricing plan (e.g., 'Clinic Starter', 'Professional', 'Enterprise').

price.description: The price of the plan, formatted as a string (e.g., '₦150,000', '$500/month').

description.description: A brief description or tagline for the pricing plan.

features.description: A list of features included in this pricing plan.

isPopular.description: Indicates whether this pricing plan is highlighted as popular.

cta.description: The call-to-action text associated with this pricing plan (e.g., 'Get Started', 'Contact Sales').

definition.description: Stores publicly available pricing plan details. Each document's ID corresponds to the plan's unique identifier (e.g., 'clinic-starter'). Read access is granted to all users (authenticated and unauthenticated). Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: planId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific pricing plan (e.g., 'clinic-starter').

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for pricing plans is a simple, top-level collection named 'pricing_plans'. Each document within this collection represents a single pricing plan, with its document ID mirroring the plan's unique identifier (e.g., 'clinic-starter', 'professional', 'enterprise'). This flat structure is ideal for data that needs to be publicly accessible and read-only for most users, while allowing administrative updates.

This structure achieves **Authorization Independence** by ensuring that all pricing plan data documents are self-contained and do not require fetching data from other collections or parent documents for read authorization. The data itself is designed to be public, meaning read access can be granted broadly without complex authorization logic or `get()` calls in security rules. For administrative write access, an independent check on a global administrator role (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`) would be performed, maintaining atomicity and avoiding hierarchical dependencies. No denormalized authorization fields are needed within the `PricingPlan` documents themselves for read access, as the data's security posture is homogeneous and publicly readable.

The structure supports **QAPs (Rules are not Filters)** because all documents within the `pricing_plans` collection share the same security requirements: publicly readable. This allows `list` queries to be secure and efficient without requiring Firestore security rules to filter results. Any user, authenticated or unauthenticated, can securely retrieve all pricing plans, as intended by the requirement for dynamic content on a landing page.
